name: Solar System
subtitle: The Sun and its eight planets
blurb: A model of our solar system — the Sun at the centre, orbited by Mercury, Venus, Earth (with the Moon), Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune, and Pluto. Tap the Sun to explore it on its own.

name: The Sun
subtitle: The star at the centre of the solar system
blurb: The Sun is the star at the centre of the solar system: a sphere of hot plasma about 1.39 million km across and roughly 4.6 billion years old, generating its energy by nuclear fusion in the core.

name: Mercury
subtitle: Closest planet to the Sun
blurb: The smallest planet and the closest to the Sun, Mercury is a cratered, nearly airless world where a single day lasts about 59 Earth days.

name: Venus
subtitle: The hottest planet
blurb: Similar in size to Earth but wrapped in a thick carbon-dioxide atmosphere, Venus is the hottest planet, with surface temperatures around 465 °C.

name: Earth
subtitle: Our home planet
blurb: Our home planet — the only known world with liquid water on its surface and life, orbited by a single large Moon.

name: Mars
subtitle: The Red Planet
blurb: The Red Planet, whose rusty colour comes from iron oxide. Mars has the tallest volcano and one of the largest canyons in the solar system.

name: Jupiter
subtitle: The largest planet
blurb: The largest planet — a giant ball of gas more than twice as massive as all the other planets combined, famous for its Great Red Spot storm.

name: Saturn
subtitle: The ringed gas giant
blurb: The ringed gas giant, circled by bright bands of icy particles and dozens of moons. It is the least dense planet — it would float in water.

name: Uranus
subtitle: The sideways ice giant
blurb: An ice giant that rotates on its side, giving it extreme seasons. Its blue-green colour comes from methane in the atmosphere.

name: Neptune
subtitle: The farthest planet
blurb: The most distant planet, a cold, windy ice giant with the fastest winds in the solar system, exceeding 2,000 km/h.
